rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references or type errors, trace the root cause to invalid identifiers or mismatched types in the same line and fix or remove the offending clause.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within AGREE annexes to avoid 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
Ensure AGREE scalar type declarations use int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid type names that cause resolution failures.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always align helper declaration types with the expression types to prevent type mismatch errors.
Avoid introducing new package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes unless they are explicitly defined in the AADL model.
When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced features and variables are valid and visible in the current scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
Assignments in implementation scopes should only reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
AGREE expressions must have consistent types on both sides of binary operators like '=' or comparison operators.
anti_patterns: Do not add new data types or package references in AGREE annexes that are not present in the AADL model.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assignments and equations there.
Do not reference identifiers in AGREE that are not declared or visible in the same annex or AADL component.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about unreachable or inadequate port types
AGREE validator diagnostics about annex placement or assume/guarantee syntax
